rule_text: AP contractor registration covers 6 categories: (i) Civil (all PWDs), (ii) Public Health Engineering, (iii) Furniture, (iv) Electrical, (v) Transport, (vi) Fabrication and Erection of structural steel — registered separately under each.
verification_method: Verify bidder registration certificate cites exact category(ies) matching tender scope

rule_text: AP contractors are graded into 6 classes (Special, I, II, III, IV, V); only Special / Class I / II / III contractors may bid State-wide; Class IV and V contractors may tender ONLY within the Circle where their registration was issued.
verification_method: Check bidder class vs work-location: Class IV/V → reject if outside their registered Circle

rule_text: Solvency Certificate for AP contractor registration is fixed at 10% of the minimum monetary limit of the registered class — issued by Tahsildar (Revenue Dept officer not below Tahsildar rank) OR a Scheduled Bank, valid for ONE YEAR from date of issue.
verification_method: Check solvency cert: amount >= 10% of class minimum AND issuing officer rank Tahsildar+ OR scheduled-bank AND age <= 1 year from issue

rule_text: AP contractor registration is valid for FIVE YEARS in any class; renewal is required after 5 years; registration window opens twice a year — January and June only.
verification_method: Check bidder registration_date is within 5 years of tender date; reject expired registrations

rule_text: Contractors of OTHER STATES must register in Andhra Pradesh BEFORE offering tender for any AP works.
condition_when: TenderState=AndhraPradesh AND TenderType IN [Works, EPC] AND BidderState != AndhraPradesh
verification_method: Check non-AP bidder has valid AP registration certificate; reject if absent

rule_text: AP Civil Works contractor monetary tendering limits by class (per GO 8/2003): Special > Rs.10 cr; Class-I Rs.2-10 cr; Class-II Rs.1-2 cr; Class-III Rs.50 lakh-1 cr; Class-IV Rs.10-50 lakh; Class-V up to Rs.10 lakh.
source_clause: GO Ms No 94/2003 Annexure-II Section 1(h) read with Annexure-III
verification_method: Check ECV against bidder's registered class limits; reject if class is below ECV band

rule_text: Demotion of an AP contractor to a lower class is permissible if he: fails to execute / executes unsatisfactorily / responsible for construction defects / lacks adequate equipment, technical personnel or financial resources / violates important contract conditions / is litigious by nature — preceded by 2-week notice for explanation.
verification_method: Check enforcement audit trail for demoted contractors: 2-week explanation period + reasoned order + reporting to Government via CE/ENC

rule_text: Suspension of business with an AP contractor (indefinite) requires: 2-week notice for explanation, prima-facie offence finding, reasoned order forwarded to Government via CE/ENC; for minor offences (e.g. failure to furnish ITC), specific-period suspension may be ordered without Government reporting.
verification_method: Check suspension order audit trail; verify suspended contractors filtered from tender eligibility
